daily_brief: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, citing more than 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths from a rare Bundibugyo strain for which no vaccines exist [BBC, Al Jazeera]. Ukraine launched one of its largest drone barrages of the conflict, with over 500 drones targeting Russian territory overnight, killing three in the Moscow region and striking a city oil refinery [BBC, France 24, The Hindu]. Tensions over Taiwan resurfaced after President Trump's Beijing summit, with Taipei firmly reasserting its sovereignty while Trump indicated he had made 'no commitment either way' and would soon decide on an $11bn weapons package [BBC, France 24, Nikkei Asia]. In counterterrorism developments, US and Nigerian forces killed ISIS global second-in-command Abu-Bilal al-Minuki in a joint operation in Nigeria's Lake Chad Basin [The Guardian, Globe and Mail]. Meanwhile, Bulgaria claimed its first Eurovision victory with Dara's 'Bangaranga,' edging out Israel in a contest marked by boycotts over Gaza [France 24, RFI, Globe and Mail]. The USS Gerald R. Ford returned home after a 326-day deployment—the longest for a US carrier since Vietnam—following operations against Iran and the capture of Venezuela's Maduro [France 24, Le Monde].

top_stories.headline: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda a Global Health Emergency
bullets: The WHO declared the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo's Ituri province and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, with approximately 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths reported
The outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo virus, a rare Ebola strain with no approved vaccines or therapeutics—only the third recorded Bundibugyo outbreak
Eight laboratory-confirmed cases exist in DR Congo, with one confirmed case in Kinshasa and two confirmed cases in neighboring Uganda, including a 59-year-old man who died Thursday
WHO warned the outbreak could be 'much larger' than currently detected, with high risk of local and regional spread due to mining towns with transient populations

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the lack of approved drugs or vaccines and risk of regional spread

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted the 88 death toll and noted this is only the third BVD outbreak ever recorded

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Earlier reporting focused on 65 deaths and Africa CDC concerns about spread

top_stories.headline: Ukraine Launches Over 500 Drones at Russia in Largest Overnight Attack, Killing Three Near Moscow
bullets: Russia's defense ministry reported intercepting 556 Ukrainian drones across 14 regions, Crimea, and the Black and Azov seas—one of the largest Ukrainian drone barrages of the war
Three civilians were killed in the Moscow region: a woman in Khimki when her house was hit, and a man and woman in the village of Pogorelki
Twelve people were injured in strikes on a Moscow city oil refinery, though authorities said the refinery's 'technology' was not damaged
Moscow's Sheremetyevo airport, Russia's busiest, reported drone wreckage on its territory but continued operations

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on casualty details and infrastructure damage

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Noted this as one of the largest Ukrainian barrages of the ongoing conflict

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the scale of Russian air defense response

top_stories.headline: Taiwan Asserts Sovereignty After Trump Declines Commitment Following Beijing Summit
bullets: Taiwan's presidential spokesperson stated it is 'self-evident' that Taiwan is 'a sovereign, independent democratic country' following Trump's remarks from Beijing
President Trump said he had 'made no commitment either way' about Taiwan after his two-day summit with Xi Jinping and would soon decide on an $11bn weapons package
Taiwan reaffirmed commitment to maintaining the status quo with China—neither declaring independence nor uniting with Beijing
The US is bound by law to provide Taiwan with means of self-defense while simultaneously maintaining diplomatic ties with China

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced reporting on both US and Taiwan positions

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Framed coverage around question of whether Chinese invasion is imminent

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Emphasized US allies' unease over Trump's transactional approach during Beijing visit

top_stories.headline: US and Nigerian Forces Kill ISIS Global Second-in-Command in Joint Operation
bullets: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, designated ISIS's global second-in-command, was killed in a joint US-Nigerian military operation in the Lake Chad Basin region of northeastern Nigeria
President Trump announced the strike, calling al-Minuki 'the most active terrorist in the world' and crediting intelligence sources for tracking his location
Nigerian President Bola Tinubu confirmed the operation killed al-Minuki along with several lieutenants in a strike on his compound
Al-Minuki, a Nigerian national, had been designated a 'specially designated global terrorist' by the US in 2023 as a Sahel-based ISIS senior leader

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Provided background on al-Minuki's 2023 designation and Trump's previous criticisms of Nigeria

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized bilateral cooperation and quoted both Trump and Tinubu statements

top_stories.headline: USS Gerald R. Ford Returns After 326-Day Deployment, Longest Since Vietnam War
bullets: The USS Gerald R. Ford, the world's largest aircraft carrier, returned to Norfolk, Virginia after a 326-day deployment—the longest for a US carrier strike group since the Vietnam War
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth welcomed the carrier home; the deployment included combat operations against Iran in the Middle East
The Ford's mission also included operations in the Caribbean involving strikes on alleged drug-smuggling boats, interdiction of sanctioned tankers, and the seizure of Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro
The carrier was originally dispatched to the Middle East ahead of the outbreak of war with Iran

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Detailed the multiple theaters of operation including Caribbean and Iran

additional_stories.headline: Bulgaria wins Eurovision Song Contest for the first time with Dara's 'Bangaranga,' with Israel finishing second amid boycott protests over Gaza
Man drives car into pedestrians in Modena, Italy, injuring eight people before emerging with a knife; suspect identified as Italian national with prior mental health referral
Israel launches airstrikes on southern Lebanon despite ceasefire extension, preceded by evacuation warnings covering nine villages
Iran announces plan to manage Strait of Hormuz traffic including tolls, will ban 'enemy' military equipment from passing through
Nepal's Kami Rita Sherpa summits Mount Everest for record 32nd time at age 56
Tata Electronics signs deal with Dutch chip equipment giant ASML to build semiconductor plant in Gujarat during PM Modi's Netherlands visit
Philippines government asks Supreme Court to allow ICC arrest of Senator Ronald Dela Rosa for drug war crimes against humanity
Tens of thousands march in London in rival demonstrations over immigration and Palestinian solidarity, with 4,000 police deployed
Pope Leo XIV to make first official papal state visit to France in 18 years in September, including stop at UNESCO headquarters
Switzerland announces it will open long-sealed intelligence files on Nazi war criminal Josef Mengele, without specifying a date
